title: Hirsutism review

examination.text: Supports documenting an abnormal hirsutism score, progression, menstrual disturbance, other hyperandrogenic features, family or ethnic context and clinician-selected androgen or 17-hydroxyprogesterone testing.
examination.rationale: The exact diagnostic section supports documenting onset, progression, impact, associated menstrual or endocrine features, relevant negatives, focused examination and already ordered endocrine laboratory results without assigning a cause.
examination.evidence_extract: Supports documenting an abnormal hirsutism score, progression, menstrual disturbance, other hyperandrogenic features, family or ethnic context and clinician-selected androgen or 17-hydroxyprogesterone testing.

source.title: Evaluation and Treatment of Hirsutism in Premenopausal Women: An Endocrine Society Clinical Practice Guideline

medication.text: Supports patient-important symptom impact, preferences, treatment-response review and clinician-led reassessment. Medicine, contraception, cosmetic procedures and treatment instructions are excluded from mappings.
medication.rationale: The exact patient-important impact and reassessment section supports patient concerns, weight context and clinician-entered review or follow-up only when discussed; it does not choose therapy.
medication.evidence_extract: Supports patient-important symptom impact, preferences, treatment-response review and clinician-led reassessment. Medicine, contraception, cosmetic procedures and treatment instructions are excluded from mappings.

source.title: Evaluation and Treatment of Hirsutism in Premenopausal Women: An Endocrine Society Clinical Practice Guideline

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 2.1–2.8 and 3.1–3.9 — patient-important impact, treatment discussion and reassessment

sources_used.title: Evaluation and Treatment of Hirsutism in Premenopausal Women: An Endocrine Society Clinical Practice Guideline
sources_used.jurisdiction: International specialty guideline; UAE laboratory, prescribing and referral policy governs local use

source_limitations: Hair distribution or score, virilisation, menstrual pattern, pregnancy possibility, medicines, family history, exact examination, androgen values, cause, treatment and monitoring remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
